All the macro and micro-nutrients are present in an easily digestible and assimilable form in milk. Milk proteins possess high biological value which is almost equal to that of meat, eggs and other high- quality animal proteins. Milk proteins are valuable supplements to most vegetarian diets. Milk is a rich source of bioavailable calcium which helps in the building up of strong bones. Milk fat serves as a vehicle for important fat-soluble vitamins A, D and E. Since, milk fat is of the saturated type those who have to be on a low fat diet can consume skimmed/toned milk. For strict vegetarians, milk is the only source of vitamin B .
